opus_warpgrep.text: The second branch of the `deleteMany` OR condition (`{ retryCount: { gt: 1 } }`) is missing a `method: WorkflowMethods.SMS` filter. This causes the SMS cron job to delete WorkflowReminder records of ALL method types (EMAIL, WHATSAPP, AI_PHONE_CALL) where retryCount > 1, not just SMS reminders. This is especially damaging because email and WhatsApp schedulers allow up to 3 retries (retryCount < 3), so reminders at retryCount === 2 would be prematurely deleted before those handlers can make their third attempt. The fix is to add `method: WorkflowMethods.SMS` to the second OR branch.

Comparison of two dayjs objects using `===` always returns `false` because `===` compares object references, not values. Should use `.isSame()`. Zero-length date overrides (where start equals end, indicating a fully blocked day) are never detected, so the slot is incorrectly treated as available.

Copy-paste bug: `end` is calculated from `slotStartTime` instead of `slotEndTime`, making `start` and `end` identical. The working hours boundary check doesn't actually verify the slot's end time. Slots that start within working hours but whose duration extends past working hours will be incorrectly shown as available.

Copy-paste error: line 114 checks `grantType` a second time instead of `rawTokenId`. `Objects.requireNonNull(grantType, "Null rawTokenId not allowed")` should be `Objects.requireNonNull(rawTokenId, "Null rawTokenId not allowed")`. This allows a null `rawTokenId` to pass constructor validation, leading to a NullPointerException later when `rawTokenId` is used (e.g., in `encodeTokenId()` or `equals()`/`hashCode()`).

Two independent bugs in `isAccessTokenId` matcher: (1) Wrong substring indices — `substring(3, 5)` extracts positions 3-4, but the grant type shortcut occupies indices 4-5, so it should be `substring(4, 6)`. (2) Inverted condition logic — `if (items[0].substring(3, 5).equals(expectedGrantShortcut)) return false;` returns false when the substring matches, but it should return false when it does NOT match. The fix is: `if (!items[0].substring(4, 6).equals(expectedGrantShortcut)) return false;`. Combined effect: the matcher always passes regardless of actual grant type, rendering all grant-type assertions useless.

The function `enableSqlExpressions` always returns `false` on every code path, making SQL expressions permanently disabled. Two compounding errors: (1) the feature flag check is inverted with `!h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(...)`, and (2) both the `if` branch and the fallthrough return `false`. The function should simply be `return h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(featuremgmt.FlagSqlExpressions)`. This causes all SQL expression queries to be rejected with "sqlExpressions is not implemented" even when the feature flag is enabled.

Swapped `dark-light-choose` arguments. The original light-mode value was `$lightness: 30%`, but the new code passes `$lightness: 70%` as the light-mode (first) argument and `30%` as the dark-mode (second) argument. This makes topic-map button links nearly invisible on light themes. Should be: `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 70%))`.

Light-mode `h3` color changed from original `$lightness: 20%` to `$lightness: 50%`, making headings significantly lighter/washed out on mobile. The desktop version correctly preserves the original `20%` for light mode. Should be: `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 20%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 80%))` to match the desktop version and preserve the original light-mode value.

Missing `slice_id` when creating per-shard `SpansBuffer` instances in `_create_process_for_shards`. The original buffer's `slice_id` is never preserved, so new buffers are created with `SpansBuffer(shards)` (slice_id=None) instead of including the slice_id. When `kafka_slice_id` is configured, spans are written to Redis keys like `span-buf:q:2-0` but the flusher looks for `span-buf:q:0`, causing silent data loss.

`time.sleep(0.1)` intended to give flusher threads time to process is a no-op because `time.sleep` was monkeypatched to `lambda _: None` earlier in the test. No actual waiting occurs, making the test potentially flaky.

Inconsistent metric tag key `"shard"` vs `"shards"` in `SpanFlusher.main`. The `wait_produce` timer uses tag key `"shards"` (plural) while all other metrics use `"shard"` (singular), causing dashboards/alerts filtering on one tag key to miss the other metric.

Django QuerySet does not support negative indexing — `queryset[start_offset:stop]` with a negative `start_offset` raises `AssertionError("Negative indexing is not supported.")`. A user can trigger this by providing a cursor with a negative offset (e.g., `cursor=0:-5:0`), which `Cursor.from_string` accepts. This results in an unhandled `AssertionError` → HTTP 500. The same issue exists in other branches where negative offsets can pass through to queryset slicing (e.g., the `cursor.is_prev=True` path in `DateTimePaginator.get_result`).

`organization_context.member` can be `None` (e.g., for SentryApp proxy users with no real org membership). When `request.user.is_superuser` is `False`, the expression `organization_context.member.has_global_access` accesses an attribute on `None`, raising `AttributeError` and causing an HTTP 500. The code should check for `None` before accessing `.has_global_access`.

Prisma's `updateMany` with an empty `data: {}` object is a no-op — no SQL UPDATE is generated, and the `@updatedAt` directive does not fire because there are no actual field changes to apply. The intent is to update `SelectedCalendar.updatedAt` for all calendars under the credential, but this will never happen. Fix: pass an explicit timestamp, e.g. `{ updatedAt: new Date() }`.

`sed -i ''` syntax is macOS/BSD-only. On GNU/Linux, `sed -i` does not take a separate backup extension argument — the `''` would be interpreted as a filename, causing the command to fail with an error. The script will fail on Linux, which is the typical CI/server environment.

Lithuanian locale file contains Italian text for `totpStep1` ("Installa una delle seguenti applicazioni sul tuo cellulare:") instead of Lithuanian. Users with Lithuanian locale will see Italian instructions for the TOTP setup step.

The `totpStep1` value uses Traditional Chinese characters (手機, 安裝, 應用程式之一) instead of Simplified Chinese (手机, 安装, 应用程序之一). The `zh_CN` locale targets Simplified Chinese users, so this will display Traditional Chinese text incorrectly.

The `error-invalid-multivalued-size` key was changed from i18next interpolation format (`{{0}}`, `{{1}}`, `{{2}}`) to Java MessageFormat (`{0}`, `{1}`, `{2}`) plus `{2,choice,...}` syntax. These files serve the React frontend which uses i18next, which requires `{{0}}` syntax. Users will see the literal uninterpolated string instead of actual values.

The contents of category_fabricator.rb and embeddable_host_fabricator.rb have been swapped. category_fabricator.rb now defines Fabricator(:embeddable_host) and embeddable_host_fabricator.rb defines the category fabricators. While Fabrication loads by symbol name so tests won't immediately break, the files contain the wrong fabricators, causing maintenance confusion.

When the 'embed_category' site setting doesn't exist (e.g., fresh installs), the SQL query returns zero rows. Accessing [0] on an empty PG::Result returns nil, and then nil['id'] raises NoMethodError. The fallback check (if category_id == 0) is never reached. Should guard against empty results, e.g., check cmd_tuples > 0 before accessing [0]['id'].

In the plural ID branch of _hydrateEmbedded, _lookupSubType returns undefined when it can't find a record, and .map() preserves these undefined values in the resulting array. The `hydrated || []` fallback is dead code since .map() always returns an array (even an empty one is truthy), so arrays of undefined values are stored. Additionally, `delete obj[k]` unconditionally removes the original _ids key even when no lookups succeeded, permanently losing the raw IDs.

The test "Should deny on explicit cache deny entry" uses `false` in the permission cache map (`"dashboards:uid:dash1": false`), which means the permission cache itself would not grant access. The test passes regardless of whether the denial cache is checked, making it vacuous. The value should be `true` so that the permission cache would grant access, thereby proving the denial cache takes priority. Without this fix, the test gives false confidence and would fail to catch a regression if the denial cache check were accidentally removed or reordered.

The `ensure_loaded!` method does not convert its `locale` argument to a symbol before checking `@loaded_locales.include?(locale)`. Since `@loaded_locales` stores symbols exclusively (via `load_locale` which calls `locale.to_sym`), passing a string locale (e.g. `"en"`) will cause the `.include?` check to always return `false` (since `"en" != :en` in Ruby), resulting in redundant `load_locale` calls on every request. The fix is to add `locale = locale.to_sym` as the first line of `ensure_loaded!`.

The `getCredentials()` method creates a `RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel` via `createFromValues()` but never sets the ID on the returned model. The stored credential has an ID (generated in `updateCredential()` at line 196), but it is not propagated to the model returned by `getCredentials()`. This results in `getId()` returning null. Downstream, when `RecoveryAuthnCodesFormAuthenticator` calls `removeStoredCredentialById(recoveryCodeCredentialModel.getId())` after all codes are used, it passes null, causing either silent failure or a NullPointerException. Fix: add `model.setId(myUser.recoveryCodes.getId());` after the `createFromValues` call.
